1.q: A locus on chromosome 7HS called HvNax3 explained up to 25% of the leaf sodium content; the suggested candidate gene underlying this locus was HVP10, a vacuolar H+-translocating pyrophosphatase.
HVP10 likely modulates Na+ sequestration into the root vacuole by acting synergistically with Na+/H+ antiporters (HvNHX1 and HvNHX4) to enhance H+ efflux and K+ maintenance in roots.
The HvNax3 locus was delimited to a 0.4 cM genetic interval, where it cosegregated with the HVP10 gene for vacuolar H+-pyrophosphatase (V-PPase).

2.q: The inactivation of HvGSK1.1 enhanced BR-dependent signaling, as indicated by the results of the leaf inclination assay in the edited lines.
The inclination angles in water-incubated line #1 was 61°... The inclination angles of line #1 in the reaction for either a single or both compounds were greater than in the non-transgenic control.

3.q: Na+/H+, counter-transporters (NHXs) not only serve as essential membrane transporters but also help catalyze the neutral exchange of K+ or Na+ for H+ and have been found to play a significant role in pH and ion homeostasis.
HVP10 likely modulates Na+ sequestration into the root vacuole by acting synergistically with Na+/H+ antiporters (HvNHX1 and HvNHX4) to enhance H+ efflux and K+ maintenance in roots.

5.q: Members of group I KT-HAK-KUP transporters play an important role in K+ acquisition by plant roots

8.q: HVA1 promotes lateral root (LR) initiation, elongation and emergence and primary root (PR) elongation via an auxin-dependent process, particularly by intensifying asymmetrical accumulation of auxin in LRP founder cells and RAM.

11.q: we demonstrated that HvCaM1 interacted with a CaM-binding transcription activator (HvCAMTA4), which may be a critical factor in the regulation of HKT1s in barley.

12.q: RNAi-mediated silencing of the target HvGSK1.1 gene was associated with modified expression of its paralogs HvGSK1.2, HvGSK2.1, HvGSK3.1, and HvGSK4.1.

13.q: Gene expression analysis revealed that HvNHX1 and HvNHX3 are the candidate genes that could have the function of regulators of ions by sequestration of Na+ in the vacuole.

15.q: HVP10 likely modulates Na+ sequestration into the root vacuole by acting synergistically with Na+/H+ antiporters (HvNHX1 and HvNHX4) to enhance H+ efflux and K+ maintenance in roots.

16.q: betaine accumulation in barley is closely related to osmotic stress, and regulated (at least in part) by changes in the expression of the BADH gene.

23.q: miR164d suppresses HvNAC92 transcription factor to directly downregulate HvHKT1;5 expression.

24.q: HvCaM1 negatively regulates salt tolerance, probably via interaction with HvCAMTA4 to modulate the down-regulation of HvHKT1;5 and/or the up-regulation of HvHKT1;1.

25.q: RT-qPCR analysis showed that HvCBP60-8 up-regulated the expression of three key ion transporter genes (HvHAK1, HvNHX1, and HvHKT1;1), resulting in improved K+/Na+ homeostasis under salt stress.

28.q: HvCaM1 negatively regulates salt tolerance, probably via interaction with HvCAMTA4 to modulate the down-regulation of HvHKT1;5 and/or the up-regulation of HvHKT1;1 to reduce shoot Na+ accumulation under salt stress.

29.q: Co-transformation of the SK-CBP60-8 (effector) construct with the RLK3/4/5_pro2k-Luc (reporter) construct in tobacco leaves enhanced luciferase activity relative to control.

32.q: Knock-down of HvHKT1;1 in barley led to higher Na+ accumulation in both roots and leaves, while overexpression of HvHKT1;1 in salt-sensitive Arabidopsis hkt1-4 and sos1-12 loss-of-function lines resulted in significantly less shoot and root Na+ accumulation.

33.q: MIR164d-OE lines showed significantly reduced root-to-shoot Na+ translocation and shoot Na+ content compared with the wild-type.

34.q: HVP10 likely modulates Na+ sequestration into the root vacuole by acting synergistically with Na+/H+ antiporters (HvNHX1 and HvNHX4) to enhance H+ efflux and K+ maintenance in roots.

35.q: Over-expression of HvPIP2;1 increased radial hydraulic conductivity of roots (Lpr) to 140%... High water-permeable activity was confirmed in Xenopus leavis oocytes expressing HvPIP2;1.

36.q: Close homology between HvITPK4 and HvITPK1 suggests that duplication of this gene occurred in barley, although this did not occur in rice.

37.q: Genes NUD (nudum) and WIN1 (wax inducer 1) of barley Hordeum vulgare are close homologs and encode AP2/ERF-type transcription factors of the WIN1/SHN1 subfamily, functionally associated with the regulation of cuticle organization.

38.q: In total, two pairs of HvPLATZ genes were segmentally duplicated (HvPLATZ3 and HvPLATZ8; HvPLATZ7 and HvPLATZ10).
